programmes.show_description: Action from the latest matches, including Coventry City v Sheffield Wednesday at Coventry Building Society Arena, Norwich City v Ipswich Town at Carrow Road and Sheffield United v Hull City at Bramall Lane in the Championship
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

The chef travels through France to visit the locations that inspired his culinary career, beginning at the Chateau Cheval Blanc in Saint-Emilion, before going back to the kitchens of the Hostellerie de Plaisance, where he worked as a lad more than 30 years ago. He rounds off his trip by cooking at an award-winning vineyard
James cooks for Strictly Come Dancing stars Amy Dowden and Carlos Gu, while there are four pasta recipes from Francesco Mazzei, and a tomato masterclass with gardener David Domoney

Jimmy Doherty and Shivi Ramoutar are joined in the kitchen by chef Paul Ainsworth, with Shivi making a Trinidad cheese roti for breakfast while Paul shares a madras roasted monkfish recipe. Plus, a masterclass in flower arranging with expert Suzie Wright, and Jimmy forages to feed the raccoons at the wildlife park before Shivi visits a cosmopolitan city food market
A round-up of the latest headlines

programmes.show_name: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programmes.show_description: Joining Alan at Manor Farm are comedian Tim Vine and actress Natalie Cassidy, while horticulturist Camilla Bassett-Smith is here with her April dairy. The programme also celebrates the best in British pasties and pints, and goes behind the scenes at Bristol Old Vic theatre

Kelvin, Liz and the kids celebrate the end of winter by hosting their very first farmers market, with people buying products from local artisans and producers, as well as the Fletchers' own wares. Marnie's lamb shack proves to be a big hit with customers queuing to be served, and Liz and Kelvin make plans to host something on a regular basis

Alan Titchmarsh and his team reveal how to bring beauty to a garden of any size, creating a stunning space filled with gorgeous blooms in Peterlee, Co Durham, for community hero, 90-year-old Mary Ann Stevenson. Taking inspiration from the pretty rose garden she would visit as a little girl, Alan transforms one part of Mary's concrete plot into a fragrant seating spot surrounded by scented roses, and Katie Rushworth transforms the centre into an elegant courtyard complete with calming water feature
Round-up of the latest headlines
Adil Ray hosts the game of words as former EastEnders star Natalie Cassidy and TV host Angellica Bell take on athletes Jonnie Peacock and Greg Rutherford for charity

Ben Shephard asks the questions as Nick Hewer, Zoe Lyons and Joe Duttine answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

Bradley Walsh hosts as Shajeev from Devon, Laura from Peebles, Sandra from Swansea and Sebastian from Northamptonshire come together in the hope that they can take on one of the experts and leave with thousands of pounds
Round-up of the latest headlines
Robert feels like he has been left with only one option, while Jacob is relieved, and Kyle goes missing
Summer quizzes Theo about Billy's final moments, Christina attempts to stop George from selling his business, and Roy's presence is undetected as the cafe goes up in smoke

Roy and Bella investigate a murder on a council estate after a teenager is stabbed to death , with signs initially pointing to it being drug-related, but the victim's parents are adamant that their son had nothing to do with drugs. The investigation brings Grace face to face with someone from his past and stirs up old demons that worry Bella, with the case placing one of the team in grave danger. Meanwhile, Branson's investigation into a masked armed robbery leads to tensions in CID
Round-up of the latest headlines

Miki Anderson returns to home town in rural Victoria after 10 years in prison and is determined to move on with her life, but a crime rocks the town again and people cannot help but be suspicious of her. Australian crime drama, starring Katherine Langford
As the townsfolk prepares for an annual celebration, Hugh's disappearance has Colleen worried and Miki hoping that she does not become the prime suspect
Daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection

Kelvin, Liz and the kids celebrate the end of winter by hosting their very first farmers market, with people buying products from local artisans and producers, as well as the Fletchers' own wares. Marnie's lamb shack proves to be a big hit with customers queuing to be served, and Liz and Kelvin make plans to host something on a regular basis
Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features
A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions
The key headlines from the day so far

Stephen Mulhern hosts as contestants Mark Bonnar, Alexandra Burke and Claire Sweeney 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice

David Dickinson and the team are in Stoke-on-Trent, where Debbie Serpell has her eyes on some Meissen and Stewart Hofgartner shows interest in novelty nutcrackers, and there is more gold for Tim Hogarth
Will the Banker be victorious as another hopeful contestant opens the iconic red boxes in a bid to take home a huge cash prize. Stephen Mulhern hosts.

Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Four people who have never met before - Josh from St Neots, Ann originally from Reading, Fergus from Glasgow and Sarah from Ipswich - take on the Chaser. In a battle of wits. Bradley Walsh guides the contestants through the show, in the hope they'll go home with thousands of pounds
By Scottish Liberal Democrats
A round-up of the day's news headlines and talking points from the UK and around the world

programmes.show_name: How to Clean Up for Cash
programmes.show_description: Josie Gibson presents as families have a loved one's clutter cleared and valued in secret by antiques expert Dan Hatfield and declutterer Jacqueline McLeod. The first mission is in Somerset, where they help Mel, who is desperate for her husband of three years Paul to rent out his house and move in with her so his clutter has to go. However, this is no easy task as Paul is a private investigator
Cain tries to convince Moira, while Sarah tries to comfort him, and new evidence is found
Todd pacifies Theo while plotting his escape, Debbie learns that Carl offered Summer a bribe, Lisa confronts Kit with Mal's accusation, and Eva tries to reunite her family

Ant and Dec host as two new campmates join the famous faces and it remains to be seen if the latest celebrities fit into camp or drama will ensue. Lots of differing opinions lead to dramatic conclusions, and the campmates continue to battle it out
A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents
Current affairs show tackling the issues behind the headlines from a Scottish perspective, with studio debate and analysis of major stories
